Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Deterministic Optimization Proxies for Efficient Single-GPU Inference
Short Hypothesis: The curvature of the optimization landscape, quantified through Hessian spectral properties, can serve as a deterministic proxy for precision allocation in mixed-precision inference, enabling resource-efficient, training-free routing decisions.
Related Work: Existing mixed-precision methods rely on heuristic calibration or static profiling, which fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. While Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) introduces geometric diagnostics based on activation covariance, it does not explicitly link curvature metrics to precision allocation. Our proposal uniquely leverages Hessian spectral properties to directly map curvature to precision tiers, providing a formalized, deterministic framework for precision routing.
Abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but often relies on heuristic or static precision allocation strategies. We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages Hessian spectral properties to deterministically allocate precision tiers. By analyzing the Hessian spectrum of hidden states, we identify regions of the optimization landscape that are robust to low-precision computation and those that require higher precision. This approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized curvature-based precision allocation, ensuring numerical stability and resource efficiency. We validate our framework on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, demonstrating superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to existing methods. Our work bridges foundational optimization theory with practical inference constraints, offering a mathematically transparent pathway for efficient mixed-precision deployment.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Hessian Spectrum Analysis - Compute the Hessian spectrum of hidden states across different layers and inputs. Evaluate the correlation between Hessian eigenvalues and quantization sensitivity.

Experiment 2: Precision Allocation - Implement a curvature-aware precision routing mechanism that maps Hessian spectral properties to precision tiers. Compare the performance against static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Experiment 3: Numerical Stability - Measure the numerical stability of the proposed framework under varying precision tiers. Use condition number and relative error as evaluation metrics.
Experiment 4: Benchmark Evaluation - Validate the framework on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks (e.g., WILDS, CIFAR-10). Assess throughput, accuracy, and resource efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The computational overhead of Hessian spectrum analysis may outweigh the benefits of precision routing.
The framework may not generalize well to architectures with highly non-convex optimization landscapes.
The deterministic nature of the precision allocation may limit adaptability to dynamic input distributions.
